items.nearby_content:
Abstract
The  ionosphere  plays  an  important  role  in  satellite  communication,  navigation systems, and space weather monitoring. However, its highly  nonlinear variability driven by solar radiation, geomagnetic activity, and  atmospheric  dynamics  makes  accurate  prediction  of  ionospheric  parameters  particularly  challenging.  In  recent  years,  deep  learning  techniques have been increasingly applied to ionospheric modelling due to  their  capability  to  learn  complex  nonlinear  relationships  from  Global  Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) signal. Despite the rapid growth of  research in this area, differences in modelling architectures, datasets, and  evaluation  methods  make  it  difficult  to  systematically  compare  model  performance across studies. This study presents a systematic review of  deep  learning  approaches  for  ionospheric  modelling  based  on  recent  studies between 2020 and 2025. The cross-study comparison was made in  terms  of  modelling  architectures,  input  features,  datasets,  prediction  targets, and reported prediction accuracy. The results show that hybrid  deep learning architectures dominate current research, while Total El

items.nearby_content: Ionosfera memainkan peranan penting dalam komunikasi satelit, sistem  navigasi, dan pemantauan cuaca angkasa. Namun begitu, variasi ionosfera  yang sangat tidak linear, yang dipacu oleh sinaran suria, aktiviti geomagnet,  serta dinamik atmosfera menjadikan ramalan parameter ionosfera satu  cabaran yang kompleks. Dalam beberapa tahun kebelakangan ini, teknik  pembelajaran mendalam semakin banyak digunakan dalam pemodelan  ionosfera kerana keupayaannya untuk mempelajari hubungan tidak linear  yang  kompleks  daripada  isyarat  Global  Navigation  Satellite  System  (GNSS). Walaupun bidang ini berkembang dengan pesat, perbezaan dari  segi  seni  bina  model,  set  data,  dan  kaedah  penilaian  menyebabkan  perbandingan prestasi model antara kajian menjadi sukar untuk dilakukan  secara sistematik.  Kajian  ini  membentangkan satu tinjauan sistematik

1.0 INTRODUCTION
The ionosphere is a highly active area of the Earth’s upper  atmosphere composed with high concentrations of ions and  abundant free electrons [1]. It located between 60 and more  than 1000 km above the Earth’s surface. This layer yielding  from  ionization  process  through  interaction  of  solar  radiation  with  atmospheric  gases,  and  also  driven  by  cosmic rays, and high energy particles from outer space [2].  The  ionized  nature  of  ionosphere  permits  reflecting,  refracting,  and  modifying  signal  propagation,  offer  numerous  significant  advantages  for  satellite-based  communication  and  navigation  systems.  Despite  these  advantages, the ionosphere poses critical problems for  contemporary space-based systems.
The  ionosphere  is  significantly  affected  by  various
physical  events,  such  as  atmospheric  tides,  seasonal  fluctuations,  geomagnetic  storms,  and  solar  extreme  ultraviolet radiation [3]. Fluctuations in ionospheric electron  density can induce scintillation effects,

This  method  facilitates  the  recognition of overarching performance trends within the  literature and offers significant insights into the advantages  and  drawbacks  of  current  deep  learning  models  for  ionospheric prediction tasks.
3.0 RESULTS AND DISCUSSION
The  preliminary  search  produced  456  records  of
published articles. After elimination 85 duplicate entries,  371 distinct articles remained for further screening. The  remaining  articles  were  screened  based  on  titles  and  abstracts to ascertained their relevance to the goals of this  review.  At  this  stage,  studies  unrelated  to  ionospheric  modelling, those not utilise deep learning approaches, and  articles concentrating on irrelevant atmospheric parameters  were removed.
Consequently, 326 papers were excluded, resulting in 45
publications for comprehensive evaluation. The remaining  publications  were  meticulously  assessed  based  on  established inclusion and exclusion criteria. Studies were  comprised if they utilised deep learning models for the
